frameworks.name: what-where-when episodic memory
frameworks.one_line: episodes decomposed into joint WWW components retrieved with distinct fidelities

frameworks.name: reconstructive memory
frameworks.one_line: remembering coherent episodes as active reconstruction from semi-independent components

frameworks.name: serial position effects
frameworks.one_line: primacy and recency shape recall accuracy of temporal information

core_question: Are the what, where, and when components of recent episodes retrieved with equal mnemonic fidelity?
key_assumptions: episodic memory can be decomposed into separable WWW components
synthetic movie stimuli expose component-wise recall on demand
mnemonic accuracy is comparable across WWW components
manipulations: probed component (what vs where vs when)
serial position of probed item within the episode
key_findings: memory for 'when' had the lowest accuracy across components
'when' recall was most affected by primacy and recency
'when' and 'where' were most susceptible to memory-load interference
'what' was relatively more robust than 'when' and 'where'
interpretations: episodes are not stored or retrieved as a coherent whole
WWW components preserve partial independence requiring active reconstruction
